releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.10.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.10.0 is a focused security, privacy, and reliability release. It came out of a deep audit of the plugin and closes a cluster of correctness gaps: a secret no longer reaches a debug log, a syncable policy can no longer smuggle a blanket command-approval wildcard, rate limits now degrade gracefully, semantic search can no longer silently misalign, and settings failures are no longer swallowed. Every change shipped failing-test-first with a permanent CI guard, so the behaviors stay fixed.

## Highlights

### Security & privacy

- **License key kept out of logs.** The chat debug request log previously captured the full request headers, including your SystemSculpt license key. The debug payload is now redacted; the real request is unchanged.
- **No blanket command approval via sync.** A Studio agent policy can no longer carry a bare `*` wildcard that would auto-approve every CLI command. The wildcard is stripped when policies are parsed, ignored by the approval gate, and scrubbed from any policy already on disk.
- **License validity downgrades only on an authoritative rejection.** A real `401`/`403` from the license service now correctly marks the license invalid, while transient/network errors preserve your last-known-good state instead of locking you out on a blip.

### Reliability

- **Rate limits degrade gracefully.** The client now honors the server's `Retry-After` on a managed `429`, classifies bring-your-own-key `429`s as transient, and performs a bounded, abort-aware retry — with a guarantee that already-streamed content can never be replayed. This is the client complement to the server-side OpenRouter hardening.
- **Streams stop when a chat view closes.** Closing a chat now aborts its in-flight request instead of leaving the network stream, token spend, and render loop running in the background.
- **Honest edit-tool results.** The filesystem agent's edit tool now reports zero or partial edits accurately instead of claiming success when nothing (or only some) of the requested changes applied, and it skips no-op writes.
- **Settings failures are surfaced.** If saving (or backing up) settings fails — disk full, permissions, a sync lock — the error is now logged and shown once via a de-duplicated notice, instead of being silently swallowed and lost on reload.

### Semantic search

- **Embedding vectors stay aligned with their chunks.** Whitespace-only or empty inputs no longer shift the returned vectors out of alignment with their source chunks, which could silently corrupt semantic-search results. The provider now returns one slot per input and asserts the counts match.

### Developer experience

- **The CSS linter is now actually enforced.** The Obsidian-override hygiene linter that ships with the repo is wired into the check gate (locally and in CI), with a guard test proving it fails on an unscoped selector — so the rule it was written to catch is finally guaranteed.
- Removed a dead token-counter code path (and its orphaned support files) that referenced a dependency that was never installed.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is required; settings migrate automatically on update.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Verification

Every change in this release landed through its own pull request with the full CI matrix green — the unit suite and built-bundle integration suite, plus the macOS and Windows end-to-end lanes — and an automated review pass. Each fix added or tightened a permanent test guard at the cheapest layer that catches the regression. The release additionally ran the native release matrix: macOS desktop baselines, the Windows clean-install and desktop-baselines GitHub end-to-end check for the released commit, and the Android sync and runtime smoke lanes.

# SystemSculpt 5.9.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.9.0 is a large feature and reliability release. It expands bring-your-own-key support to Claude and Gemini through their native APIs, makes BYOK users first-class (no license wall in chat), hardens startup and updates on every platform, and overhauls the embeddings and audio-transcription pipelines. It also lands the clearer license-renewal experience and a batch of chat and agent-mode improvements.

## Highlights

### Providers (bring your own key)

- **Claude (Anthropic) BYOK** via the native Messages API — add your Anthropic key and use Claude models directly in Chat.
- **Gemini (Google) BYOK** via the native `generateContent` API.
- A connected provider's models can no longer silently disappear from the Chat picker: a model-catalog status state machine keeps the dropdown honest.
- **BYOK users never hit a license wall.** When you run on your own provider key, chat no longer demands a SystemSculpt license, and a provider-key auth failure is reported as a provider problem ("Open Providers") rather than a subscription/renewal prompt.

### Platform reliability

- More reliable startup on Android and Linux, including a Node-free mobile bundle-load path so the plugin initializes without desktop-only APIs.
- A disabled plugin is now fully inert — no timers, listeners, or background work linger after you turn it off.
- Versioned settings migration with update safety: settings are migrated forward deterministically, with safe-mode and minimum-version fail-soft so a bad load can't wedge the plugin.

### Embeddings

- A portable embeddings index that survives Obsidian Sync and vault restore — export/import, snapshots, and automatic restore.
- Typed provider errors and more robust response parsing.
- Automatic retry with backoff for transient provider errors and rate limits.
- A bulk rebuild that can resume after an interruption even when auto-processing is off.

### Recording & transcription

- A typed transcription provider core: typed errors, a response normalizer, and an HTTP classifier shared across providers.
- **Self-hosted Whisper** support with config-time validation and a settings UI.
- A separate, configurable post-processing model for cleaning up transcripts.
- Captured audio is saved when iOS interrupts a recording (lock screen / phone call), so meetings aren't lost.
- Mobile recording bitrate is capped so long meetings stay within transcription limits.
- One-tap stop for the recorder — the stop control no longer needs repeat taps.
- YouTube transcription now returns an actionable message when the transcript service is over quota, instead of leaking a raw error.

### Chat & agent mode

- Agent mode is scoped to the individual chat instead of a global setting.
- The local Pi agent loop stops cleanly on repeated identical failing tool calls and surfaces an actionable error when a tool-use turn stalls.
- Favorite models are restored in the composer model picker.
- Filesystem agent tools run on mobile, and Folder Notes paths resolve correctly across all file tools.
- A per-chat toggle to hide system and tool messages.
- "Attach Context File" is available from the slash palette.

### License experience

- A clear, actionable renewal flow for expired or invalid SystemSculpt licenses, replacing the old generic stream error — with distinct copy and actions for expired vs. invalid vs. provider-key states.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is required; settings migrate automatically on update.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.
- BYOK users: if you previously saw a license prompt while using your own provider key, that no longer happens — chat runs on your key.

## Verification

Plugin checks, the full unit suite, and the built-bundle integration suite passed locally. The release also ran the native release matrix: macOS desktop baselines, the Windows clean-install and desktop-baselines GitHub end-to-end check for the released commit, and the Android sync and runtime smoke lanes. Every change in this release landed through CI with the macOS and Windows E2E lanes green on its pull request.

# SystemSculpt 5.8.1

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.8.1 is an OpenAI provider setup patch. Adding an OpenAI API key now makes GPT-5.4 Mini available in the Chat model picker, and chat requests for it route to OpenAI using your key.

This continues the provider setup work from 5.7.1, which fixed the same kind of "provider connected but its model is missing from Chat" gap for xAI.

## Highlights

- A connected OpenAI provider now adds GPT-5.4 Mini to the Chat model picker.
- Chat requests for that model route to OpenAI's API with your own key.
- Added a guard so a configured provider's model cannot silently disappear from the picker again.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- If you already added an OpenAI key but its model did not appear, update the plugin, then refresh provider status or reconnect OpenAI.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Fixed OpenAI bring-your-own-key models not appearing in the Chat model dropdown after the provider was configured.
- Added a regression guard that checks every model offered in the picker has a working execution endpoint, so this class of dropdown gap is caught before release.

## Verification

Plugin checks, unit tests, and the built-bundle checks passed locally. The changes in this release also passed the macOS desktop baselines and the Windows clean-install and desktop-baselines end-to-end checks in continuous integration. Android and iOS device validation were not run for this patch.

releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.8.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.8.0 adds image generation to the desktop Studio. A Studio image node turns a text prompt into images through SystemSculpt's hosted image service, with direct control over the model, aspect ratio, image size, seed, and how many images to generate per run.

This release also removes the legacy CanvasFlow feature, which decorated Obsidian's native canvas and was superseded by Studio.

## Highlights

- Generate images from a Studio image node: choose the model, aspect ratio, image size, seed, and batch count (up to 4).
- Leave the seed blank for random results, or set a whole number for reproducible output.
- The model picker only lists models that can actually generate images, so you never select one the service rejects.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- CanvasFlow has been removed; the desktop Studio view replaces it. Any saved `canvasFlowEnabled` setting is cleaned up automatically on upgrade.
- Image generation is a desktop-only Studio feature.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Image generation requests normalize cleanly: camelCase and snake_case option names are both accepted, and an image size the chosen model does not support is dropped instead of failing the request.
- Idempotent retries of an image job stay stable across the image-size change, so a repeated request replays the original job instead of returning a conflict.
- Selecting an unsupported image model now returns a clear invalid-model response.

## Verification

Plugin checks, unit tests, release surface checks, the macOS desktop baselines, the Windows clean-install and desktop-baselines GitHub check, and the Android sync + runtime smoke passed before release. iOS device validation was not run for this release.

## API and Platform Work Included

- Backend image-generation request schema hardening (camelCase option aliases, image-size-aware idempotency hashing, explicit invalid-model errors), deployed to api.systemsculpt.com ahead of this release.
- Removed the legacy CanvasFlow modules and relocated the shared image-generation modules to `src/services/images/`.

# SystemSculpt 5.7.1

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.7.1 is an xAI provider setup patch. Connecting an xAI API key now makes Grok 4.3 available in the Chat model picker, and the Providers tab gives you a direct `Use in Chat` action once the provider is ready.

This release also makes provider disconnect and reconnect behavior more reliable, so a saved API key is reflected consistently after refreshes and Obsidian restarts.

## Highlights

- Providers now show when a connected provider has a ready chat model.
- xAI now adds Grok 4.3 to the model picker after you save a valid API key.
- The xAI row now includes `Use in Chat`, which opens a new chat with Grok 4.3 selected.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- If xAI was already connected but Grok did not appear, update the plugin, restart Obsidian, then refresh provider status or reconnect xAI.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Fixed a case where xAI could show as connected while Grok models were missing from Chat.
- Fixed provider API-key persistence so saved and cleared keys survive refreshes and restarts more consistently.
- Improved the Providers tab so connected providers point directly to the model users can try next.
- Added Windows release validation for the fresh-install provider setup path.

## Verification

Plugin checks, unit tests, release surface checks, a real Obsidian desktop xAI/Grok chat smoke, and the native release gate passed before release.

## API and Platform Work Included

- Added the xAI remote provider endpoint and Grok 4.3 catalog entry used by provider-authenticated chat.
- Added a Windows release check that runs a fresh Obsidian install before release.

# SystemSculpt 5.7.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.7.0 is a Studio and chat reliability release. You can now paste or drag media files straight into Studio, so screenshots and images land on the canvas without a detour through your vault. Long, tool-heavy chats are also better at recovering their final answer when a turn gets interrupted mid-stream.

Managed models now pick up their names, limits, and capabilities from the SystemSculpt server, so the model list stays current without waiting for a plugin update.

## Highlights

- Studio now accepts pasted and dropped media files directly onto the canvas, including images from your clipboard.
- Chat now recovers interrupted turns more reliably, so long tool-driven replies are less likely to lose the final answer after a stream hiccup.
- Chat keeps its streaming indicator consistent through repeated tool calls in a single turn, so the view reflects what the model is actually doing.
- Managed models now sync their metadata from the server, so new models and updated limits appear without a plugin update.
- Support links now point to the SystemSculpt support page.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- Existing chats, settings, and Studio boards continue to work as before.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Fixed cases where an interrupted chat turn could leave the conversation stuck instead of resuming or surfacing the partial reply.
- Hardened tool-loop handling so repeated tool calls in one turn keep the streaming indicator and final message consistent.
- Model metadata now falls back to the bundled catalog when the server is unreachable, so offline use keeps working.

## Verification

Plugin checks, the unit test suite, and the release surface gate passed before tagging.

releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.6.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.6.0 is a focused Search release. The in-vault Search modal now opens faster, keeps results steady while the content index catches up, and handles punctuated, multilingual, and symbol-only queries that used to drop out of results. Recent notes show up immediately for empty queries, and their snippets stay fresh after you edit a file.

If you use Obsidian's "Excluded files" filters, or you have embeddings enabled, this release also fixes several cases where Search used to disagree with your current settings until you reloaded.

## Highlights

- Search now opens with an instant recents list and starts returning metadata matches before the full content index finishes building, so the first results land almost immediately.
- Search now stays stable while the content index refreshes, so notes you are reading stop popping in and out of results between keystrokes.
- Search now finds punctuated queries like `orange-juice`, `don't`, and `co-op` against notes that contain the exact punctuated phrase, instead of silently dropping those hits after the index is ready.
- Search now handles multilingual and symbol-only queries more reliably, including CJK (e.g. `東京`), Cyrillic, and emoji-only terms.
- Recent notes now pick up fresh previews after an edit instead of showing a stale snippet until the next full search.
- Search now respects Obsidian's "Excluded files" filter the moment you change it, both for recents and for live search results.
- Smart mode now lazily starts the embeddings manager when embeddings are enabled, so users with auto-processing off still get semantic hits without a manual reload.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- Existing chats, settings, and search behavior should continue to work as before.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Fixed a case where changing Obsidian's "Excluded files" filter only took effect for the first change, leaving later edits ignored until an unrelated vault event.
- Fixed a case where starting a smart search during a background index build could leave excluded files searchable even after the filter had changed.
- Fixed short prefix queries (e.g. typing `fr` on the way to `fresh`) so they keep returning matches after the content index is ready.
- Fixed the empty-query recents list so it drops newly excluded notes and shows updated previews for recently modified files.
- Fixed a case where smart mode silently fell back to lexical-only when embeddings were enabled but the embeddings manager had not been initialized yet.

## Verification

Plugin checks, unit tests (5301 passing), and the release surface gate passed. CI `unit` and `desktop-baselines` checks are green on the merged PR.

# SystemSculpt 5.5.1

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.5.1 is a small Studio polish and update-safety patch. Studio node selection now reads more clearly when a node has expanded controls, and the release path has stricter checks so future updates are less likely to ship with mismatched version files or missing plugin assets.

## Highlights

- Studio now wraps the selected and hovered node glow around expanded top and bottom controls, not just the center card.
- Studio no longer uses the old left-edge run-status stripe, so selection and hover states are easier to read.
- Release checks now verify the plugin version surfaces, public release notes, ignored build artifacts, and the exact Obsidian asset set before publishing.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual action is needed.
- Existing chats, settings, and Studio projects should continue to load normally.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Fixed selected Studio nodes with expanded controls so the accent border reads as one continuous shape.
- Improved hover and selection feedback while keeping active node dragging lighter.
- Added release-surface validation so future patch releases catch version drift and missing assets before a GitHub release is created.

releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.5.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.5.0 improves Studio, chat reliability, and provider setup. The Studio canvas now has a stronger foundation for larger graph workspaces, with expanded navigation bounds, steadier viewport behavior, cleaner node-card rendering, and connection handling built for bigger layouts.

Provider setup is clearer too. The Providers tab now does a better job separating subscription login, saved API keys, and local Pi provider setup, including local Ollama guidance. Chat also recovers more clearly when streaming or provider errors interrupt a response.

## Highlights

- Studio now supports much larger graph workspaces with improved canvas bounds, viewport behavior, node-card rendering, and connection handling.
- Chat now gives clearer feedback when streaming fails or a provider connection needs attention.
- Provider setup now explains local Pi configuration, saved API-key state, and subscription login state more directly.
- Pi provider authentication is more resilient when local auth storage is temporarily unavailable.
- Direct provider agent/tool turns are more reliable after tool calls, especially for Chat Completions-compatible providers.
- Release and review automation received behind-the-scenes hardening for safer future updates.

## Upgrade Notes

- No manual vault migration is expected.
- Existing chats, provider settings, and Studio data should continue to load normally.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version remains `1.4.0`.
- Local Pi provider setup still depends on the configured Pi `models.json` path.

## Fixes and Reliability

- Improved chat recovery after streaming, quota, auth, and provider disconnect errors.
- Improved provider disconnect persistence and cleanup behavior.
- Improved local provider setup tests and fallback behavior for Pi auth inventory.
- Improved release tooling so reviewed version metadata can be prepared before tagging.
- Improved automated review validation used during development.

releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.4.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.4.0 restores three chat controls that were removed during the v4.15 codebase unification, redesigned from scratch. Users now have explicit control over agent behavior, system prompts, and model favorites directly from the chat composer.

## Highlights

- **Agent Mode Toggle** — A new bot icon in the chat composer toolbar lets you switch between agent mode (tools + file operations) and pure chat (no tools, no agent system prompt). When OFF, the AI focuses entirely on reasoning without risk of unintended file modifications. The toggle state persists per-chat and to your global settings.

- **System Prompt Selector** — A prompt chip in the composer toolbar opens a selection modal for vault-based system prompts. Drop `.md` files into your `SystemSculpt/System Prompts` folder and pick them per-conversation. Custom prompts are orthogonal to agent mode: with agent mode ON, your personality prompt is composed with tool-usage instructions so tools still work. With agent mode OFF, just your prompt is sent. Per-chat persistence included.

- **Favorites Filter Restored** — The star filter button is back in the model selection modal. Clicking it filters the list to show only your favorited models.

- **Cross-Platform Build Fix** — Resolved a plugin crash on desktop (`Cannot find module 'proper-lockfile'`) and mobile (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'Stream')`). Node-only transitive dependencies are now externalized with try/catch wrapping so they degrade gracefully across all platforms.

## Important Notes

- Agent mode defaults to ON, preserving current behavior. Existing users won't notice any difference until they click the toggle.
- System prompts are stored as regular vault markdown files with optional YAML frontmatter for description and icon. No new file formats or databases.
- The `SystemSculpt/System Prompts` folder is the same directory the plugin has always used. If you had prompt files there before v4.15, they'll appear in the selector automatically.
- No server-side changes were needed. The SystemSculpt API is a transparent pass-through for system prompts and tool control.

## Verification

- 5238 unit tests passing (382 suites)
- macOS desktop E2E baselines passing (CI)
- Android emulator smoke tests passing (chat, file-read, file-write, embeddings, web-fetch)
- Windows VM plugin loads without crash
- Three rounds of automated PR review feedback addressed

## Testing Infrastructure

- New `npm run check:pre-push` command orchestrates unit tests, build, Android smoke, and Windows baselines in one pass
- Android trust prompt automation via CDP
- Claude Code pre-push hook gates every push with unit tests + build

releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.3.3

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.3.3 is a chat reliability patch focused on two failure shapes in the assistant timeline. The visible fix is that reasoning, tool calls, and content now stay in true emitted order while a response is streaming and after a saved chat is reopened. The deeper fix is that managed SystemSculpt turns no longer lose the final answer when hosted tool-call ids repeat across continuation rounds.

## Highlights

- ChatView now preserves chronological assistant output instead of collapsing reasoning blocks together or bunching tool calls away from the content they belong to.
- Persisted chats now reload in the same order they were emitted, so a saved conversation keeps its reasoning, tool-use, and response fragments aligned.
- Managed SystemSculpt continuations now remap repeated hosted tool-call ids per assistant occurrence, which prevents later tool results from attaching to the wrong round and restores final answer delivery for long tool-driven turns.
- The regression surface is now locked down with targeted coverage for renderer order, reasoning layout, markdown round-trips, hosted continuation remapping, and desktop automation reopen flows for saved chat history.
- Release preflight is also cleaner in this patch line: the Windows runtime version fixture no longer contains a tracked machine-specific path that would block a local release.

## Important Notes

- This is a patch release focused on correctness and continuity in ChatView. It does not introduce a new top-level product surface.
- No manual migration is expected. Existing chats and saved history should simply render and continue more reliably after updating.
- The missing-final-answer bug was isolated to the managed SystemSculpt hosted transcript path. A provider-backed parity rerun on `local-pi-openrouter@@openai/gpt-5.4-mini` still completed successfully, which is why the fix is scoped to hosted continuation remapping instead of changing the Pi/provider execution path.
- The native release matrix passed on macOS, Windows, and Android during this release pass. iOS runtime smoke remains optional and unavailable in this environment because no physical iOS device was attached.

## Verification

The `5.3.3` release pass completed these local validation gates on March 31, 2026:

- `npm run check:plugin`
- `npm test`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run check:release:native`

`npm run check:release:native` passed the full native release contract:

- macOS desktop baselines
- Windows clean-install parity
- Windows desktop baselines
- Android sync plus headless relaunch
- Android extended runtime smoke

The Android extended runtime smoke completed chat, filesystem read/write, embeddings, transcription, record-and-transcribe, web fetch, and YouTube transcript checks in the same release pass.

## API and Platform Work Included

- Assistant timeline rendering now keeps reasoning, tool calls, and content in the same chronological order across live streaming and saved-chat reloads.
- Chat markdown persistence now round-trips interleaved assistant parts without flattening them into the wrong sequence on reopen.
- Managed hosted continuation requests now allocate unique API tool-call ids for repeated upstream tool ids and bind tool messages back to the correct assistant occurrence.
- Desktop automation helpers can now reopen saved chat history directly, which gives this failure mode a durable live repro and regression-proof path.
- Release safety preflight stays green against tracked machine-specific Windows test fixtures, keeping the local `gh` release lane shippable.

releases.author: SystemSculpt
releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.3.2

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.3.2 is a reliability and UX patch for the Pi-backed plugin workflow. The headline change is that local Pi tool calls now stay on the local Pi path end to end instead of being mistaken for hosted follow-up work, while a separate round of user-reported UX regressions around transcription, ribbons, Studio open, and chat visibility is also cleaned up in the same release line.

## Highlights

- Local Pi tool executions now surface in ChatView as completed local tool calls, with their final result attached, instead of being reinterpreted as hosted external tool requests on the next turn.
- The provider-connected native desktop baseline now exercises a real filesystem tool turn on `local-pi-openrouter@@openai/gpt-5.4-mini` on both macOS and Windows, including completed tool-call assertions rather than only checking response text.
- Studio provider inventory now reads the canonical bundled Pi auth storage directly. The old runtime-relative fallback that could silently hide broken production reads in the built plugin is gone.
- Windows release prep now auto-refreshes stale plugin runtimes through the canonical remote bootstrap relaunch path and redacts sensitive bootstrap report fields before printing them.
- Meeting processor outputs now stay associated with renamed audio files, so a processed note does not quietly fall back to an unprocessed state after the source recording is renamed.
- The transcription clean-up prompt setting is restored, mobile recording now prefers `m4a` when the platform supports it, chat can hide system-role messages again, Studio can auto-create a project on first open, and the ribbon lane is less intrusive.

## Important Notes

- This is a patch release focused on reliability, release confidence, and UX polish. It does not add a new top-level product surface.
- Provider-connected verification for the Pi lane remains OpenRouter-first in this environment, using `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` as the supported release-check target.
- The auth-inventory change is intentionally strict: if the canonical bundled Pi auth path breaks again, the failure should surface instead of being hidden behind a silent fallback.
- `npm run check:release:native` passed on macOS, Windows, and Android during this release pass. iOS runtime smoke remains optional and unavailable in this environment because no physical iOS device was attached.
- No manual migration should be required for existing users beyond the normal plugin update.

## Verification

The `5.3.2` release candidate passed these local gates on March 31, 2026:

- `node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs testing/native/device/windows/bootstrap.test.mjs testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs testing/native/device/windows/run-desktop-automation.test.mjs testing/native/device/windows/run-clean-install-parity.test.mjs testing/native/device/windows/remote-run.test.mjs`
- `bash autoresearch.sh`
- `npm test`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run check:plugin`
- `SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" npm run test:native:desktop:provider-connected`
- `SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" npm run test:native:windows:provider-connected`
- `tmpfile=$(mktemp) && npm run test:native:windows:prepare -- --launch > "$tmpfile" && rg -n '"licenseKey"|"userEmail"|"displayName"|\[REDACTED\]' "$tmpfile"`
- `npm run check:release:native`
- `git diff --check`
- `gitleaks git . --log-opts='5.3.1..HEAD'`

The provider-connected filesystem tool baseline is green on both macOS and Windows, with the expected fixture output and `completedRelevantToolCallCount=3` on the OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini Pi path.

`npm run check:release:native` also reran the broader native release contract and passed macOS desktop baselines, Windows clean-install parity, Windows desktop baselines, Android sync plus headless relaunch, and Android extended runtime smoke. On the Windows bootstrap path, the final release pass confirmed that sensitive seeded fields print as `[REDACTED]` rather than leaking local license or identity data.

## API and Platform Work Included

- Local Pi streaming now preserves completed tool-call state and tool results from `tool_execution_end` instead of inferring completion from a later stop reason.
- ChatView streaming and continuation logic now respects already-completed local Pi tool calls instead of trying to continue them through the hosted tool loop.
- Native desktop automation baselines now assert real provider-connected filesystem tool turns and completed tool-call counts on macOS and Windows.
- Windows clean-install and desktop-automation runners now compare the live bridge plugin version against the repo's `manifest.json` and automatically run the canonical remote bootstrap relaunch when the runtime is stale.
- Windows bootstrap output now redacts license, identity, and token-like fields before printing, and provider-connected automation classifies the Win32 console-title pipe error as a transient candidate retry instead of a deterministic plugin failure.
- Studio provider auth inventory now uses the bundled Pi auth storage directly, so release builds do not silently diverge from the canonical runtime path.
- UX fixes in this release line include meeting-processor rename tracking, restored transcription prompt controls, mobile recorder format preference cleanup, less aggressive ribbon behavior, first-open Studio project creation, and restored system-message visibility controls in chat.

releases.author: SystemSculpt
releases.description:
# SystemSculpt 5.3.1

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.3.1 is a native release-hardening patch for the Pi-backed plugin workflow. The headline change is that release verification now runs through one real macOS, Windows, and Android matrix, and the provider-connected Pi lane uses the supported OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini path instead of stale direct-OpenAI assumptions.

## Highlights

- Added a first-class native release matrix that gates macOS desktop baselines, Windows clean-install parity, Windows desktop baselines, Android sync and headless relaunch, and Android runtime smoke before draft creation.
- Hardened the Windows no-focus automation lane so transient hosted send failures are classified separately from real plugin regressions, with combined baseline summaries and explicit managed recovery checks.
- Defaulted provider-connected desktop and Windows parity checks to OpenRouter using `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, which matches the supported Pi-connected provider path in this environment.
- Tightened Pi auth storage, vault-path resolution, Studio provider inventory, and desktop automation bridge behavior so Settings, Studio, and native automation all read from the same bundled auth and storage model.
- Scrubbed machine-specific tracked paths from release-facing fixtures, examples, and remote temp defaults so the release safety preflight now passes cleanly before tag or draft creation.

## Important Notes

- This is a patch release focused on reliability, validation, and release safety. It does not introduce a new end-user product surface.
- Provider-connected release validation is intentionally OpenRouter-first for the Pi lane in this environment. Direct OpenAI provider credits are not part of the supported release path here.
- iOS runtime smoke was intentionally skipped in this release pass because no physical iOS device was connected. macOS, Windows, and Android all passed.
- No manual migration should be required for existing users beyond the normal plugin update.

## Verification

The `5.3.1` release candidate passed these gates locally on March 30, 2026:

- `npm test`
- `npm run check:plugin`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run check:release:native`
- `node scripts/release-plugin.mjs --dry-run --bump patch`

The native release matrix passed across:

- macOS desktop baselines
- Windows clean-install parity
- Windows desktop baselines
- Android sync and headless relaunch
- Android runtime smoke

## API and Platform Work Included

- Native release-gate runner and coverage wired into plugin shipping
- Windows bootstrap, remote-run, clean-install, and no-focus desktop automation harness improvements
- Provider inventory and model selection updates for the OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini Pi lane
- Pi auth storage and bundled desktop support cleanup across plugin runtime and Studio
- Release-flow hardening around tracked local-only files, machine-specific paths, and GitHub draft creation auth fallback

# SystemSculpt 5.3.0

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.3.0 focuses on the Pi-powered chat experience and the native desktop automation lane we use to prove it. The main theme is reliability through simplification: cleaner chatview model switching, better transcript continuity when moving between SystemSculpt and local Pi, and a no-focus Obsidian automation path that is sturdier under reload churn.

## Highlights

- Chatview model switching is cleaner and easier to maintain, with the picker flow split into clearer ownership boundaries instead of more logic living inline in the main view.
- Transcript sync now preserves the non-Pi portion of a conversation when switching between SystemSculpt and local Pi, instead of collapsing the visible chat down to only the newest Pi turn.
- The desktop automation bridge now waits for a stable bridge generation, recovers more cleanly from bridge rollovers, and has dedicated chatview stress coverage for repeated model-switch and reload churn.
- Duplicate embeddings status bars are now cleaned up during reloads so repeated development or automation cycles do not leave the footer stacked with stale entries.
- External `data.json` sync now has a polling fallback in addition to `fs.watch`, so the no-focus bridge recovery path is less dependent on one watcher mechanism behaving perfectly on the live desktop host.

## Important Notes

- This release is intended to ship using the native no-focus desktop automation path under `testing/native/`, not the older renderer-driving desktop approach.
- The new external-settings polling fallback requires one manual plugin reload in an already-open vault the first time this build is introduced. After that, the steady-state goal is fully no-focus recovery and verification.
- Local automated verification is already green on the current tree, and the live attach-only desktop revalidation is now green on the already-open target vault after a clean quit/reopen.

# SystemSculpt 5.1.1

## What's New

SystemSculpt 5.1.1 focuses on the reliability issues users feel first: mobile transcription parity, clearer large-upload failures, and a release path grounded in the native Obsidian runtime instead of older drift-prone test infrastructure.

This release also finishes the move to the shared native smoke harness as the canonical integration path for shipping the plugin, so the lane we maintain is the same lane we trust for release verification.

## Highlights

- SystemSculpt transcription now uses the hosted jobs pipeline on mobile as well as desktop, bringing the two environments into closer parity.
- Audio uploads now make chunking decisions based on estimated multipart request size rather than raw blob size alone, which better protects near-limit recordings from provider-side size failures.
- Plain-text `413` upload errors now surface directly instead of being wrapped in a confusing JSON parse failure.
- The shared `testing/native/` runtime smoke harness is now the canonical release-validation path, replacing the older WDIO-era testing lane and retiring stale release plumbing that no longer matched the real plugin workflow.

## Important Notes

- `5.1.0` remains an unpublished historical draft state. `5.1.1` is the public release built from the current verified plugin tree.
- Desktop release validation passed through the extended native runtime smoke lane, including chat, file read/write, embeddings, transcription, record-and-transcribe, web fetch, and YouTube transcript coverage.
- Android native runtime smoke passed the early extended checks used in this release pass, but the Android embeddings validation lane still needs follow-up and remains a known gap rather than a claimed green lane.
- Windows support remains documented, but Windows has not yet received a fresh live host validation pass for this release.

## Verification

The `5.1.1` release candidate passed the following release gates on the committed tree:

- `git diff --check`
- `npm run check:plugin:fast`
- `npm test`
- focused transcription test coverage for hosted jobs parity, multipart-aware chunking, and plain-text `413` handling
- desktop extended native runtime smoke
- `node scripts/release-plugin.mjs --dry-run --version 5.1.1`

Desktop extended native runtime smoke covered:

- exact-answer chat
- file read
- file write
- embeddings
- transcription
- record and transcribe
- live web fetch
- YouTube transcript retrieval

## API and Platform Work Included

- Hosted SystemSculpt transcription jobs now apply consistently on mobile and desktop.
- Upload handling now supports desktop file handles when available and vault-backed bytes when running on mobile.
- Custom-provider chunking now uses estimated multipart request size rather than only the raw blob size.
- Provider upload failures now preserve clearer plain-text error messages when that is what the upstream service returns.
- Native runtime smoke under `testing/native/` is now the maintained release contract for integration verification.

## What's New in 5.0.0

SystemSculpt AI 5.0.0 is a major Pi-first release for the desktop plugin.
The headline change is that SystemSculpt now behaves like a normal Pi provider on desktop, so vault-aware chat, setup, and model selection all follow one canonical path instead of splitting between local and remote execution models.

### Highlights

- SystemSculpt now runs through the local Pi RPC path on desktop, using the same vault-aware execution model as the other Pi providers.
- Removed the old remote plugin session transport and moved the managed API side to the standard `/api/v1/chat/completions` contract.
- Cleaned up SystemSculpt model naming and setup copy so the provider reads naturally in the picker, active chat state, and migration flows.
- Hardened Pi bootstrap and setup flows, including bundled runtime verification and first-run guidance for users who do not already have Pi installed.
- Improved Studio with stronger terminal/runtime packaging, better graph interaction behavior, more reliable project/run handling, and cleaner Pi-auth-aware model selection.
- Tightened embeddings reliability across batching, provider switching, and production API handling.

### Important Notes

- This release is intentionally desktop-first for the SystemSculpt Pi experience. If Pi is not installed yet, the plugin will guide users through setup instead of falling back to the retired remote text path.
- Existing SystemSculpt chat selections now migrate onto the canonical local Pi model path.
- The update checker continues to use the plugin latest-version endpoint, so once this release is published users should receive the normal update notification flow.

### Verification

- Full plugin release checks passed locally, including TypeScript, build, tests, Pi runtime asset generation, and runtime verification.
- Focused release-gate tests passed for the update checker, Pi setup/bootstrap, SystemSculpt provider behavior, embeddings batch handling, and the website chat/embeddings contract.
- Production smoke checks passed for:
  - `/api/v1/chat/completions`
  - `/api/plugin/embeddings`
  - `/api/plugin/plugins/systemsculpt-ai/latest`

### API and Platform Work Included Since 4.15.0

- Replaced the old plugin session APIs with the canonical chat completions endpoint.
- Kept live SystemSculpt chat and embeddings healthy in production while aligning the plugin and website contracts.
- Synced the website repository to the corresponding API-side checkpoint for this release line.

### TL;DR
Agent tools now execute immediately without requiring manual approval, removing all configuration complexity.

## What Changed

### Automatic Tool Execution
Agent tools (file reading, writing, searching, etc.) now run automatically without asking for confirmation. Previously, certain "mutating" operations like writing files required you to click "Approve" before they would execute. This friction has been completely removed.

### Simplified Settings
The Agent Tools settings section has been removed from the Chat settings tab. There are no longer any toggles to configure which tools require approval, because none of them do. Tools simply work.

### Always-Available Internal Tools
The built-in filesystem and YouTube tools are now always available when using agent mode. They no longer depend on any MCP settings being enabled. If a model supports tool use, the tools will work.

### Smaller Plugin Size
Removed approximately 1,000 lines of approval-related code, including the approval deck UI, approval panels, and associated CSS. This makes the plugin slightly smaller and removes unused code paths.

## Technical Notes
- Quick Edit mode no longer checks deprecated MCP settings
- Custom MCP servers still respect their individual enable/disable setting
- If a model doesn't support tools, they gracefully degrade at runtime

# SystemSculpt v4.4.6 — Critical Fix for Agent Tools

### TL;DR
Fixes "Tool is disabled" errors that prevented filesystem and YouTube tools from working even after updating to 4.4.5.

## Fixed

**Agent tools now work without any settings configuration**

The previous release (4.4.5) made internal tools available in the tool list, but missed the execution path. This caused tools to appear available but fail with "Tool is disabled: mcp-filesystem:list_items" errors when actually used.

Internal servers (filesystem, YouTube) now bypass ALL settings checks during both listing AND execution.

## What This Means For You

- Filesystem tools (read, write, search, list) work immediately
- YouTube transcript tools work immediately
- No need to enable anything in settings
- No more "Tool is disabled" errors for built-in tools

releases.author: SystemSculpt
releases.description:
# SystemSculpt v4.4.5 — Smarter Model Compatibility & Always-On Agent Tools

### TL;DR
Models that don't support agent tools now gracefully fall back to standard chat, and core agent features are always available without manual setup.

## Automatic Fallback for Incompatible Models

When you select a model that doesn't support agent tools (like some OpenRouter models), SystemSculpt now handles this gracefully:

- **Auto-retry without tools**: If a model rejects agent tool requests, the message is automatically retried without tools
- **Clear feedback**: A footnote appears on the response: "Response generated without agent tools"
- **Remembered for next time**: The incompatibility is saved so future messages skip tools automatically
- **Warning banner**: A banner appears to let you know the current model has limited capabilities

This means no more error messages when using models that don't support function calling — the chat just works.

## Agent Tools Always Available

The built-in agent tools (file operations, YouTube transcripts) are now always available:

- No need to enable MCP in settings for core features
- Filesystem and YouTube tools work out of the box
- Custom MCP servers still respect their individual enable/disable settings

## New /agent-status Command

Type `/agent-status` in the chat to see:

- Which agent tools are currently available
- Whether your selected model supports tools
- Compatibility status and any limitations

## Embeddings License Handling

Improved behavior when license validation fails:

- No longer spams retry attempts on license errors
- Automatically resumes when you fix your license
- Clearer distinction between temporary errors and license issues
